titel: The Commission has approved state aid to Romania's Investment and Development Bank.
html: European Commission Press Release, Brussels, June 26, 2026. The European Commission has approved, in accordance with EU rules on state aid, Romanian measures concerning the national investment and development bank, Banca de Investiții și Dezvoltare S.A. ('BID').
tldr: The European Union has approved a measure allowing the Romanian state to provide financial support to its national investment bank. This means the bank can receive additional funds to help with development initiatives in Romania.
lattlast:
The European Commission, the executive body of the EU, has approved state aid for the Romanian bank Banca de Investiții și Dezvoltare. This decision means that the bank will receive support from the Romanian government in accordance with EU regulations.

This support is intended to help the bank in its work on investments and development in Romania. With this approval, the bank can allocate more resources to promote economic growth in the country.

The decision, which was made on June 26, 2026, allows the Romanian authorities to proceed with their plans for the bank. This is an important part of how the EU ensures that member states can support their economies legally.
